name: Kamakhya
shortDescription: Kamakhya is a significant Hindu Tantric goddess of desire, often associated with the yoni and fertility. Her temple in Assam, India, is a major pilgrimage site for Tantrics and devotees of Shakti.
description: Kamakhya Devi is a prominent Hindu goddess, particularly revered in Tantric traditions. She is often seen as a form of Shakti, the divine feminine energy, and is closely associated with the yoni (female genitalia) and the power of creation. The Kamakhya Temple in Guwahati, Assam, India, is a major pilgrimage destination dedicated to her worship and is considered one of the most important Shakti Peethas (seats of divine power). The temple celebrates the annual Ambubachi Mela, a festival marking the goddess's menstruation, during which the Brahmaputra River near the temple turns red. This event symbolizes the goddess's fertility and creative power. The mythology surrounding Kamakhya often links her to Shiva, highlighting the dynamic interplay between the masculine and feminine principles. She is also connected to the ten Mahavidyas, the wisdom goddesses in Tantric Hinduism. Kamakhya represents the power of desire, both as a source of worldly attachment and as a path to spiritual liberation. Her worship involves complex rituals and practices aimed at harnessing her energy for personal transformation and enlightenment. She is a powerful symbol of female empowerment and the sacredness of the female body within Hindu Tantric traditions, embodying both the creative and destructive aspects of the divine feminine.

sources.title: The Goddess in India: The Five Faces of the Eternal Feminine
sources.author: Devi, Prabhavananda

sources.title: Hindu Goddesses: Visions of the Divine Feminine in the Hindu Religious Tradition
sources.author: Kinsley, David R.
